title: NiftiExtension Class — NiftiExtension-class
text: NIfTI-1.1 extensions follow structure: Bytes 0-3: esize (int32) - total extension size, must multiple 16 Bytes 4-7: ecode (int32) - extension code identifying format Bytes 8-(esize-1): edata - actual extension data Extensions chained sequentially NIfTI header (byte 352) vox_offset reached.

title: NiftiExtension Class — NiftiExtension-class
text: ecode integer extension code identifying type extension. See NiftiExtensionCodes known codes. esize integer giving total size extension bytes, including 8-byte header (esize + ecode). Must multiple 16. edata raw vector containing extension data (length = esize - 8).

title: NiftiExtension Class — NiftiExtension-class
code: # Create a simple comment extension comment_text <- "This is a test comment" ext <- NiftiExtension(ecode = 6L, data = comment_text)  # Access the extension code ext@ecode #> [1] 6

title: Create a NIfTI Extension — NiftiExtension
text: Constructor function creating NiftiExtension-class object proper padding ensure size multiple 16 bytes.

title: Create a NIfTI Extension — NiftiExtension
code: NiftiExtension(ecode, data)

title: Create a NIfTI Extension — NiftiExtension
text: ecode Integer extension code. See NiftiExtensionCodes known codes. Common values: 4 (AFNI), 6 (comment), 32 (CIFTI). data extension data. Can : character string (converted raw null terminator) raw vector (used -)

title: Create a NIfTI Extension — NiftiExtension
text: NiftiExtension-class object.

title: Create a NIfTI Extension — NiftiExtension
text: function automatically handles padding ensure total extension size (esize) multiple 16 bytes, required NIfTI specification. esize includes 8-byte header (esize + ecode fields).

title: Create a NIfTI Extension — NiftiExtension
code: # Create a comment extension ext <- NiftiExtension(ecode = 6L, data = "This is a comment") ext@ecode #> [1] 6 ext@esize #> [1] 32  # Create an AFNI extension with XML data afni_xml <- '<?xml version="1.0"?><AFNI_attributes></AFNI_attributes>' afni_ext <- NiftiExtension(ecode = 4L, data = afni_xml)

title: Known NIfTI Extension Codes — NiftiExtensionCodes
text: named integer vector registered NIfTI extension codes. codes identify format/type extension data.

title: Known NIfTI Extension Codes — NiftiExtensionCodes
code: NiftiExtensionCodes

title: Known NIfTI Extension Codes — NiftiExtensionCodes
text: Named integer vector names describe extension type: ignore 0 - Unknown/private format (recommended) DICOM 2 - DICOM format (attribute tags values) AFNI 4 - AFNI group (ASCII XML attributes) comment 6 - Plain text comment XCEDE 8 - XCEDE format jimdiminfo 10 - JIM dimension info workflow_fwds 12 - Workflow forwards FreeSurfer 14 - FreeSurfer format pypickle 16 - Python pickle MiND_ident 18 - MiND identifier b_value 20 - B-value (diffusion) spherical_direction 22 - Spherical direction DT_component 24 - DT component SHC_degreeorder 26 - SHC degree order voxbo 28 - VoxBo format Caret 30 - Caret format CIFTI 32 - CIFTI format variable_frame_timing 34 - Variable frame timing eval 38 - Eval MATLAB 40 - MATLAB format Quantiphyse 42 - Quantiphyse MRS 44 - MRS NIfTI

title: Known NIfTI Extension Codes — NiftiExtensionCodes
code: # Get the code for AFNI extensions NiftiExtensionCodes["AFNI"]  # Returns 4 #> AFNI  #>    4   # Get the name for a code names(NiftiExtensionCodes)[NiftiExtensionCodes == 4]  # Returns "AFNI" #> [1] "AFNI"

title: SparseNeuroVec Class — SparseNeuroVec-class
code: # Create a sparse 4D brain image mask <- LogicalNeuroVol(array(runif(64*64*32) > 0.7, c(64,64,32)), NeuroSpace(c(64,64,32))) data <- matrix(rnorm(sum(mask) * 100), nrow=sum(mask), ncol=100) sparse_vec <- SparseNeuroVec(data=data, mask=mask, space=NeuroSpace(dim=c(64,64,32,100)))  # Access a subset of the data subset <- sparse_vec[,,, 1:10]   bspace <- NeuroSpace(c(10,10,10,100), c(1,1,1)) mask <- array(rnorm(10*10*10) > .5, c(10,10,10)) mat <- matrix(rnorm(sum(mask)), 100, sum(mask)) svec <- SparseNeuroVec(mat, bspace, mask) length(indices(svec)) == sum(mask) #> [1] TRUE

title: Affine utility functions — affine_utils
text: Utilities point transforms affine decomposition/composition. Applies homogeneous affine transform points coordinate axis last dimension. Useful expanding affine include additional dimensions. arrays `, B, C, ...`, returns `Returns per-output-axis obliquity relative cardinal axes, radians. Preserves rotations/shears world location center voxel.

title: Affine utility functions — affine_utils
code: apply_affine(aff, pts, inplace = FALSE)  to_matvec(transform)  from_matvec(matrix, vector = NULL)  append_diag(aff, steps, starts = numeric(0))  dot_reduce(...)  voxel_sizes(affine)  obliquity(affine)  rescale_affine(affine, shape, zooms, new_shape = NULL)

title: Affine utility functions — affine_utils
text: aff Base affine matrix. pts Points last dimension `N - 1`; shape may vector, matrix, higher-dimensional array. inplace Included API compatibility; ignored R. transform Homogeneous transform matrix. matrix Linear part transform (`N x M`). vector Optional translation vector length `N`. steps Diagonal values appended dimensions. starts Optional translations appended dimensions. ... Matrices/vectors compatible %*%. affine Square homogeneous affine matrix. shape Original grid shape (spatial axes). zooms New voxel sizes spatial axes. new_shape Optional new grid shape; defaults `shape`.

title: Affine utility functions — affine_utils
text: Points transformed `aff`, leading shape `pts`. list `matrix` `vector`. Homogeneous affine matrix shape `(N+1) x (M+1)`. Expanded affine matrix. Matrix product. Numeric vector voxel sizes (column norms linear block). Numeric vector obliquity angles. Rescaled affine matrix.

code: # Create a simple 3D volume with random values space <- NeuroSpace(c(10,10,10), spacing=c(1,1,1)) vol <- NeuroVol(array(runif(1000), c(10,10,10)), space)  # Create a mask by thresholding (values > 0.5 become TRUE) mask1 <- as.mask(vol > 0.5)  # Create a mask by specifying indices indices <- which(vol > 0.8)  # get indices of high values mask2 <- as.mask(vol, indices)  # Both masks are LogicalNeuroVol objects identical(class(mask1), class(mask2)) #> [1] TRUE

what: Apply a 4D bilateral filter to a NeuroVec — bilateral_filter_4d
title: Apply a 4D bilateral filter to a NeuroVec — bilateral_filter_4d
text: function applies full 4D bilateral filter NeuroVec, smoothing jointly across space (x, y, z) time (t). filter uses spatial, temporal, intensity kernels preserve edges reducing noise, leveraging parallel C++ backend performance.

title: Apply a 4D bilateral filter to a NeuroVec — bilateral_filter_4d
code: bilateral_filter_4d(   vec,   mask,   spatial_sigma = 2,   intensity_sigma = 1,   temporal_sigma = 1,   spatial_window = 1,   temporal_window = 1,   temporal_spacing = 1 )

title: Apply a 4D bilateral filter to a NeuroVec — bilateral_filter_4d
text: vec NeuroVec object (4D image). mask optional LogicalNeuroVol NeuroVol specifying spatial region process. omitted, entire spatial extent processed. spatial_sigma Numeric; standard deviation spatial Gaussian (default 2). intensity_sigma Numeric; standard deviation intensity Gaussian (default 1). temporal_sigma Numeric; standard deviation temporal Gaussian (default 1). spatial_window Integer; half-width spatial window voxels (default 1), e.g., 1 => 3x3x3 spatial neighborhood. temporal_window Integer; half-width temporal window frames (default 1), e.g., 1 => 3 timepoints (t-1, t, t+1). temporal_spacing Numeric; spacing temporal dimension (e.g., TR seconds). Default 1. sets temporal scale used temporal kernel.

title: Apply a 4D bilateral filter to a NeuroVec — bilateral_filter_4d
text: NeuroVec filtered data.

title: Apply a 4D bilateral filter to a NeuroVec — bilateral_filter_4d
text: Parameter guidance units: - spatial_sigma: Measured physical units (millimeters). Distances   computed using spacing(vec)[1:3], choose spatial_sigma   relative voxel size. rule thumb, set 1-2 voxel sizes   (e.g., 2-4 mm 2 mm isotropic data) moderate smoothing. - intensity_sigma: Dimensionless multiplier global intensity standard   deviation. Internally, filter uses exp(-(dI)^2 / (2 * (intensity_sigma * sigma_I)^2)),   sigma_I standard deviation finite voxel intensities within   mask across time. Start 1.0 moderate smoothing; use 0.5-0.8   preserve edges, 1.5-2.0 stronger smoothing. - temporal_sigma: Measured temporal_spacing units (e.g., seconds).   Typical values 0.5-2 x TR. Larger values blend across time. Choosing neighborhood window sizes: - spatial_window controls discrete spatial support. common choice   ceiling(2 * spatial_sigma / min(spacing(vec)[1:3])), covers   ~95 - temporal_window similarly can set ceiling(2 * temporal_sigma / temporal_spacing). Quick presets (typical fMRI 2-3 mm voxels TR~2s): - Light: spatial_sigma = 1 x min(spacing), intensity_sigma = 0.8,   temporal_sigma = 0.5 x TR, windows = 1 - Moderate (default-ish): spatial_sigma = 1.5 x min(spacing), intensity_sigma = 1.0,   temporal_sigma = 1 x TR, windows = 1-2 - Strong: spatial_sigma = 2 x min(spacing), intensity_sigma = 1.5,   temporal_sigma = 1.5 x TR, windows = 2 Tip: time axis known TR, pass via temporal_spacing. NIfTI inputs, can get TR via:
code: hdr <- read_header(nifti_path)   tr  <- hdr@header$pixdim[5]   out <- bilateral_filter_4d(vec, mask, temporal_spacing = tr)

title: Apply a 4D bilateral filter to a NeuroVec — bilateral_filter_4d

title: Correlation-guided bilateral filtering (convenience wrapper) — cgb_filter
code: cgb_filter(   runs,   mask = NULL,   spatial_sigma = 2,   window = NULL,   corr_map = c("power", "exp", "soft"),   corr_param = 2,   topk = 16L,   passes = 1L,   lambda = 1,   leave_one_out = FALSE,   run_weights = NULL,   add_self = TRUE,   time_weights = NULL,   confounds = NULL,   robust = c("none", "huber", "tukey"),   robust_c = 1.345,   return_graph = FALSE )

title: Correlation-guided bilateral filtering (convenience wrapper) — cgb_filter
text: runs NeuroVec list NeuroVec. mask Optional LogicalNeuroVol/NeuroVol logical array spatial masking. Defaults -mask voxels. spatial_sigma Spatial Gaussian sigma mm. Used weighting , window NULL, auto-choose neighborhood size. window Integer half-width cubic neighborhood. NULL, computed ceiling(2 * spatial_sigma / min(spacing)) least 1. corr_map Mapping pooled correlation affinity; one "power", "exp", "soft". Defaults "power". corr_param Parameter corr_map (gamma/tau/r0 respectively). topk Keep strongest k neighbors (0 keeps ). Defaults 16. passes Number smoothing passes (>=1). Defaults 1. lambda Blend factor [0,1] per pass. Defaults 1 (pure diffusion). leave_one_out TRUE multiple runs supplied, builds LORO graphs returns list smoothed runs. run_weights Optional numeric weights per run Fisher-z pooling. add_self Logical; add tiny self-edge normalization. time_weights Optional list (single vector) per-run time weights. confounds Optional list (single matrix) per-run confounds. robust One "none", "huber", "tukey". robust_c Tuning constant robust weights. return_graph Logical; TRUE, also return graph(s) alongside smoothed data.

title: Correlation-guided bilateral filtering (convenience wrapper) — cgb_filter
text: leave_one_out=FALSE, smoothed NeuroVec.   leave_one_out=TRUE, list smoothed NeuroVec.   return_graph=TRUE, returns list elements result   graph (single object lists accordingly).

title: Correlation-guided bilateral filtering (convenience wrapper) — cgb_filter
text: convenience front-end cgb_make_graph cgb_smooth bilateral-like interface: - window NULL, chosen   ceiling(2 * spatial_sigma / min(spacing)) (least 1). Larger   windows allow correlations distant neighbors, cost   extra compute memory. - spatial_sigma (mm) controls quickly spatial weights fall   distance. Small values emphasize local structure; larger values   mix information wider spatial footprint. - corr_map corr_param set pooled correlations   turned edge weights:   * "power": (r) = r^gamma r > 0. Larger     gamma (e.g., 3-4) strongly emphasizes high correlations     produces edge-preserving, patchy smoothing; smaller values (e.g., 1-2)     behave like standard correlation-weighted smoothing.   * "exp": Gaussian 1 - r scale tau. Small     tau keeps similar time-series; larger tau makes     filter closer spatial Gaussian still respecting sign.   * "soft": (r) = max(r - r0, 0). Increasing r0     discards weak correlations tends sharpen edges can make     result piecewise-constant. - topk limits voxel k strongest neighbors.   Smaller topk yields sparser, anisotropic graphs (cheaper   sometimes less smooth); larger topk increases mixing memory. - passes lambda control diffusion strength.   lambda = 1, pass applies pure graph diffusion; multiple passes   compound smoothing. Choosing lambda < 1 blends pass   identity can prevent -smoothing using passes. - Setting leave_one_out = TRUE multi-run inputs builds separate   graph run excludes correlations, reduces   information leakage cross-validation decoding workflows. - time_weights, confounds, robust/robust_c   adjust time-points contribute correlation estimates. -   weighting high-motion/high-DVARS frames (via make_time_weights   robust != "none") typically yield smoother, less noisy graphs   can also reduce effective temporal degrees freedom. - Use return_graph = TRUE plan reuse constructed   graph(s) cgb_smooth inspect sparsity pattern.

title: Correlation-guided bilateral filtering (convenience wrapper) — cgb_filter
code: # \donttest{ vec <- read_vec(system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package = "neuroim2")) mask <- read_vol(system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package = "neuroim2"))  # Auto window from spatial_sigma and spacing, single pass out <- cgb_filter(vec, mask, spatial_sigma = 3, window = NULL, topk = 16)  # Stronger diffusion with two passes and lambda < 1 out2 <- cgb_filter(vec, mask, spatial_sigma = 4, window = NULL,                    passes = 2, lambda = 0.7) # }

what: Build a correlation-guided bilateral (CGB) graph — cgb_make_graph
title: Build a correlation-guided bilateral (CGB) graph — cgb_make_graph
text: Computes sparse row-stochastic graph whose weights combine spatial proximity pooled local time-series correlations. Supports optional censoring weights, nuisance regression via weighted QR projectors, leave-one-run-graph construction, robust -weighting high-DVARS volumes.

title: Build a correlation-guided bilateral (CGB) graph — cgb_make_graph
code: cgb_make_graph(   runs,   mask = NULL,   window = 1L,   spatial_sigma = 2,   corr_map = c("power", "exp", "soft"),   corr_param = 2,   topk = 16L,   leave_one_out = FALSE,   run_weights = NULL,   add_self = TRUE,   time_weights = NULL,   confounds = NULL,   robust = c("none", "huber", "tukey"),   robust_c = 1.345 )

title: Build a correlation-guided bilateral (CGB) graph — cgb_make_graph
text: runs NeuroVec list NeuroVec objects (typically one per run). mask Optional LogicalNeuroVol/NeuroVol logical array defining -mask voxels. Defaults -mask voxels. window Integer half-width cubic spatial neighborhood (e.g., 1 yields 3x3x3 window). spatial_sigma Spatial Gaussian sigma mm. corr_map Mapping pooled correlation affinity; one "power", "exp", "soft". "power" "soft" mappings rectify negative correlations, whereas "exp" preserves (useful sharpening smoothing). corr_param Parameter chosen corr_map (gamma, tau, r0 respectively). topk Keep strongest k neighbors masking (0 keeps ). leave_one_out Logical; TRUE multiple runs provided, returns list graphs run u excludes correlations. run_weights Optional numeric weights per run used Fisher-z pooling. Defaults \(n_k - 3\) (usable frames minus three) omitted. add_self Logical; always inject tiny self-edge normalization. time_weights Optional list (single vector) per-run time weights \(w_t \[0,1]\) applied correlation estimation. intercept always included correlations computed weighted, demeaned series. confounds Optional list (single matrix) per-run confound regressors project prior correlation estimation. robust One "none", "huber", "tukey"; "none" additional DVARS-style reweighting applied. robust_c Tuning constant robust weights (Huber/Tukey).

title: Build a correlation-guided bilateral (CGB) graph — cgb_make_graph
text: list containing row_ptr, col_ind, val,   dims3d, mask_idx, (leave_one_out=TRUE)   list graphs.

title: Build a correlation-guided bilateral (CGB) graph — cgb_make_graph
text: Graph construction overview: - Neighborhood: -mask voxel , consider cubic spatial window   half-width window (.e., (2*window+1)^3 candidates). Candidates   outside mask bounds ignored. - Spatial kernel: candidate j physical distance d_ij (mm), assign   spatial weight w_s = exp(-d_ij^2 / (2 * spatial_sigma^2)). Distances use   spacing(spatial_space). - Correlation pooling: Compute Pearson correlation r_k(,j) within run k   (optionally nuisance projection/weights), transform Fisher-z,   pool across runs weights \(\omega_k\) (default \(n_k - 3\)),   back-transform r_pool via tanh. - Correlation--affinity mapping (corr_map):   * "power" (mode=0): (r) = r^gamma r>0 else 0. Parameter = gamma.   * "exp" (mode=1): (r) = exp(- (1 - r)^2 / (2 * tau^2)). Parameter = tau.   * "soft" (mode=2): (r) = max(r - r0, 0). Parameter = r0. - Combined weight: w_ij = w_s(,j) * (r_pool(,j)). topk > 0, keep   strongest topk neighbors. Optionally inject small self-edge   add_self=TRUE. Finally, row-normalize obtain stochastic W. Parameter guidance: - spatial_sigma mm. typical choice 1-2x voxel size   (e.g., 2-4 mm 2 mm isotropic). Larger values increase spatial mixing. - window controls support; good rule   ceiling(2 * spatial_sigma / min(spacing)). - corr_map: use "power" corr_param = 2 robust   smoothing; "exp" tau ~ 0.5-1.5 retains sign information;   "soft" r0 ~ 0.1-0.3 thresholds weak correlations. - topk: 8-32 practical range; higher values densify graph   increase compute/memory. - leave_one_out: multi-run inputs, enabling prevents run   using correlations building graph (mitigates leakage). Nuisance/time weights/robust options: - confounds/time_weights specified (robust != "none"),   per-run weighted QR projectors used; correlations computed   projected, weighted series. Robust options add DVARS-like -weighting. - request implicit intercept (actual confounds, time   weights, robust), baseline builder used identical results. Output usage: - Returns CSR arrays (row_ptr, col_ind, val) define   row-stochastic matrix W masked voxels. Apply cgb_smooth. - Complexity scales number masked voxels times neighborhood size   (limited topk). Memory proportional number retained edges.

title: Cluster-centroid searchlight over cluster time-series — cluster_searchlight_series
text: x `ClusteredNeuroVec` object `NeuroVec` plus `cvol` cvol `ClusteredNeuroVol` (required `x` `NeuroVec`) k Integer, number nearest clusters including seed (default: 10). capped total number clusters specified value exceeds . radius Numeric distance mm. given, use clusters within radius instead k-nearest neighbors. used together k. label Optional character label returned windows

title: Cluster-centroid searchlight over cluster time-series — cluster_searchlight_series

title: Cluster-centroid searchlight over cluster time-series — cluster_searchlight_series
text: function creates searchlight around cluster's centroid, selecting either: k nearest clusters (k specified) clusters within given radius (radius specified) particularly useful cluster-level connectivity analyses working parcellated data voxel-level searchlights redundant.

title: Cluster-centroid searchlight over cluster time-series — cluster_searchlight_series
code: # Create synthetic 4D data (8x8x8 volume, 10 timepoints) sp4 <- NeuroSpace(c(8,8,8,10), c(1,1,1)) arr <- array(rnorm(8*8*8*10), dim=c(8,8,8,10)) vec <- NeuroVec(arr, sp4)  # Create a mask covering most of the volume sp3 <- NeuroSpace(c(8,8,8), c(1,1,1)) mask_arr <- array(FALSE, dim=c(8,8,8)) mask_arr[2:7, 2:7, 2:7] <- TRUE mask <- LogicalNeuroVol(mask_arr, sp3)  # Assign voxels to 10 clusters n_voxels <- sum(mask_arr) clusters <- sample(1:10, n_voxels, replace=TRUE) cvol <- ClusteredNeuroVol(mask, clusters)  # Create clustered representation cv <- ClusteredNeuroVec(vec, cvol)  # Get cluster searchlight with 3 nearest neighbors windows <- cluster_searchlight_series(cv, k = 3) length(windows)  # 10 windows (one per cluster) #> [1] 10  # Check first window roi1 <- windows[[1]] dim(values(roi1))  # 10 x 3 (timepoints x neighbors) #> [1] 10  3  # Use radius-based neighborhoods (5mm radius) windows_radius <- cluster_searchlight_series(cv, radius = 5) # Each window may have different number of neighbors

title: Connected components — conn_comp
text: x image object ... additional arguments including: threshold - numeric value defining lower intensity bound image mask cluster_table - logical indicating whether return cluster statistics local_maxima - logical indicating whether compute local maxima local_maxima_dist - minimum distance local maxima connect - connectivity pattern ("26-connect", "18-connect", "6-connect") threshold threshold defining lower intensity bound image mask cluster_table return cluster_table local_maxima return table local maxima local_maxima_dist distance used define minum distance local maxima

title: Connected components — conn_comp
text: list containing: index - ClusteredNeuroVol object cluster labels size - NeuroVol object cluster sizes voxels - list cluster voxel coordinates cluster_table - (optional) Data frame cluster statistics local_maxima - (optional) Matrix local maxima coordinates object representing connected components x.

title: Connected components — conn_comp
code: # Create a simple 3D volume with two distinct regions space <- NeuroSpace(c(10,10,10), c(1,1,1)) vol_data <- array(0, c(10,10,10))  # Create first cluster in corner (2x2x2) vol_data[1:2, 1:2, 1:2] <- 1  # Create second cluster in opposite corner (2x2x2) vol_data[8:9, 8:9, 8:9] <- 1  # Create NeuroVol object vol <- NeuroVol(vol_data, space)  # Find connected components with default 26-connectivity # Returns components above threshold 0 comps <- conn_comp(vol, threshold=0)  # Access results max(comps$index) == 2  # Should have 2 clusters #> [1] TRUE all(comps$size >= 0)    # All clusters should have >= 0 #> [1] TRUE  # Get cluster statistics comps <- conn_comp(vol, threshold=0, cluster_table=TRUE) # cluster_table contains: index, x, y, z, N (size), Area, value  # Find local maxima within clusters comps <- conn_comp(vol, threshold=0, local_maxima=TRUE,                   local_maxima_dist=2) # local_maxima contains: index, x, y, z, value

what: Extract Connected Components from a 3D Binary Mask — conn_comp_3D
title: Extract Connected Components from a 3D Binary Mask — conn_comp_3D
text: Identifies labels connected components 3D binary mask using two-pass algorithm. function supports different connectivity constraints returns component indices sizes.

title: Extract Connected Components from a 3D Binary Mask — conn_comp_3D
code: conn_comp_3D(mask, connect = c("26-connect", "18-connect", "6-connect"))

title: Extract Connected Components from a 3D Binary Mask — conn_comp_3D
text: mask 3D logical array representing binary mask connect character string specifying connectivity constraint. One "26-connect" (default), "18-connect", "6-connect"

title: Extract Connected Components from a 3D Binary Mask — conn_comp_3D
text: list following components: index 3D array integers. non-zero value represents cluster index                connected component voxel. Zero values indicate background. size 3D array integers. non-zero value represents size (number voxels)               connected component voxel belongs . Zero values indicate background.

title: Extract Connected Components from a 3D Binary Mask — conn_comp_3D
text: function implements efficient two-pass connected component labeling algorithm: First pass: Assigns provisional labels builds equivalence table using         union-find data structure label resolution Second pass: Resolves label conflicts assigns final component labels connectivity options determine voxels considered adjacent: 6-connect: face-adjacent voxels (±1 step along axis) 18-connect: Face edge-adjacent voxels 26-connect: Face, edge, vertex-adjacent voxels (neighbors 3x3x3 cube) Time complexity O(n) n number voxels mask, additional O(k) space union-find data structure k number provisional labels.

title: Extract Connected Components from a 3D Binary Mask — conn_comp_3D
text: Rosenfeld, ., & Pfaltz, J. L. (1966). Sequential operations digital picture processing. Journal ACM, 13(4), 471-494.

title: Extract Connected Components from a 3D Binary Mask — conn_comp_3D
code:
# Create a simple 3D binary mask with two disconnected components mask <- array(FALSE, c(4, 4, 4)) mask[1:2, 1:2, 1:2] <- TRUE  # First component mask[3:4, 3:4, 3:4] <- TRUE  # Second component  # Extract components using different connectivity patterns comps <- conn_comp_3D(mask, connect = "6-connect")  # Number of components max_comps <- max(comps$index) cat("Found", max_comps, "components
") #> Found 2 components  # Size of each component unique_sizes <- unique(comps$size[comps$size > 0]) cat("Component sizes:", paste(unique_sizes, collapse=", "), "
") #> Component sizes: 8   # Try with different connectivity comps_26 <- conn_comp_3D(mask, connect = "26-connect") cat("Number of components with 26-connectivity:", max(comps_26$index), "
") #> Number of components with 26-connectivity: 1

title: Downsample an Image — downsample
text: x DenseNeuroVol object downsample ... Additional arguments passed specific downsample methods. spacing Target voxel spacing (numeric vector length 3) factor Downsampling factor (single value vector length 3, 0 1) outdim Target output dimensions (numeric vector length 3) method Downsampling method (currently "box" box averaging)

title: Downsample an Image — downsample
text: object class x, downsampled according specified parameters.

title: Downsample an Image — downsample
code: # Create a sample 4D image data <- array(rnorm(64*64*32*10), dim = c(64, 64, 32, 10)) space <- NeuroSpace(dim = c(64, 64, 32, 10),                      origin = c(0, 0, 0),                     spacing = c(2, 2, 2)) nvec <- DenseNeuroVec(data, space)  # Downsample by factor nvec_down1 <- downsample(nvec, factor = 0.5)  # Downsample to target spacing nvec_down2 <- downsample(nvec, spacing = c(4, 4, 4))  # Downsample to target dimensions nvec_down3 <- downsample(nvec, outdim = c(32, 32, 16))  # Create a sample 3D volume data <- array(rnorm(64*64*32), dim = c(64, 64, 32)) space <- NeuroSpace(dim = c(64, 64, 32),                      origin = c(0, 0, 0),                     spacing = c(2, 2, 2)) vol <- DenseNeuroVol(data, space)  # Downsample by factor vol_down1 <- downsample(vol, factor = 0.5)  # Downsample to target spacing vol_down2 <- downsample(vol, spacing = c(4, 4, 4))  # Downsample to target dimensions vol_down3 <- downsample(vol, outdim = c(32, 32, 16))

title: Generic function to position kernel in a position in image space — embed_kernel
code: # Create a 3D Gaussian kernel with dimensions 3x3x3 and voxel size 1x1x1 kern <- Kernel(kerndim = c(3,3,3), vdim = c(1,1,1), FUN = dnorm, sd = 1)  # Create a NeuroSpace object to embed the kernel in space <- NeuroSpace(c(10,10,10), c(1,1,1))  # Embed the kernel at the center of the space (position 5,5,5) embedded_kern <- embed_kernel(kern, space, c(5,5,5))  # The result is a SparseNeuroVol with kernel weights centered at (5,5,5) # We can also scale the kernel weights by using the weight parameter embedded_kern_scaled <- embed_kernel(kern, space, c(5,5,5), weight = 2)  # The scaled kernel has weights twice as large as the original max(values(embedded_kern_scaled)) == 2 * max(values(embedded_kern)) #> [1] TRUE

title: Gaussian Blur for Volumetric Images — gaussian_blur
text: vol NeuroVol object representing image volume smoothed. mask optional LogicalNeuroVol object representing image mask. mask defines region blurring applied. provided, entire volume processed. sigma numeric value specifying standard deviation Gaussian kernel. Default 2. window integer specifying kernel size. represents number voxels include side center voxel. example, window=1 results 3x3x3 kernel. Default 1.

title: Gaussian Blur for Volumetric Images — gaussian_blur
text: NeuroVol object representing smoothed image.

title: Gaussian Blur for Volumetric Images — gaussian_blur
text: function uses C++ implementation efficient Gaussian blurring. blurring applied voxels within specified mask (entire volume mask provided). kernel size determined 'window' parameter, shape 'sigma' parameter.

title: Gaussian Blur for Volumetric Images — gaussian_blur
text: Gaussian blur: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gaussian_blur

title: Gaussian Blur for Volumetric Images — gaussian_blur
code: # Load a sample brain mask brain_mask <- read_vol(system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package = "neuroim2"))  # Apply Gaussian blurring to the brain volume blurred_vol <- gaussian_blur(brain_mask, brain_mask, sigma = 2, window = 1)  # View a slice of the original and blurred volumes image(brain_mask[,,12])  image(blurred_vol[,,12])

title: Generic function to convert N-dimensional grid coordinates to 1D indices — grid_to_index
code: # Create a 2D space (10x10) space_2d <- NeuroSpace(c(10,10), c(1,1))  # Convert 2D grid coordinates to linear indices coords_2d <- matrix(c(1,1, 2,2), ncol=2, byrow=TRUE) idx_2d <- grid_to_index(space_2d, coords_2d) # First coordinate (1,1) maps to index 1 # Second coordinate (2,2) maps to index 12 (= 2 + (2-1)*10)  # Create a 3D space (10x10x10) space_3d <- NeuroSpace(c(10,10,10), c(1,1,1))  # Convert 3D grid coordinates to linear indices coords_3d <- matrix(c(1,1,1, 2,2,2), ncol=3, byrow=TRUE) idx_3d <- grid_to_index(space_3d, coords_3d)  # Single coordinate can also be converted idx <- grid_to_index(space_3d, c(1,1,1))  slice_space <- NeuroSpace(c(10, 10)) slice_data <- matrix(1:100, 10, 10) slice <- NeuroSlice(slice_data, slice_space)  # Convert single coordinate idx <- grid_to_index(slice, c(5, 5))  # Convert multiple coordinates coords <- matrix(c(1,1, 2,2, 3,3), ncol=2, byrow=TRUE) indices <- grid_to_index(slice, coords)

what: Edge-Preserving Guided Filter for Volumetric Images — guided_filter
title: Edge-Preserving Guided Filter for Volumetric Images — guided_filter
text: function applies guided filter volumetric image (3D brain MRI data) perform edge-preserving smoothing. guided filter smooths image preserving edges, providing balance noise reduction structural preservation.

title: Edge-Preserving Guided Filter for Volumetric Images — guided_filter
code: guided_filter(vol, radius = 4, epsilon = 0.7^2)

title: Edge-Preserving Guided Filter for Volumetric Images — guided_filter
text: vol NeuroVol object representing image volume filtered. radius integer specifying spatial radius filter. Default 4. epsilon numeric value specifying regularization parameter. controls degree smoothing edge preservation. Default 0.49 (0.7^2).

title: Edge-Preserving Guided Filter for Volumetric Images — guided_filter
text: NeuroVol object representing filtered image.

title: Edge-Preserving Guided Filter for Volumetric Images — guided_filter
text: guided filter operates computing local linear models guidance image (input image implementation) output. 'radius' parameter determines size local neighborhood, 'epsilon' controls smoothness filter. implementation uses box blur operations efficiency, approximates behavior original guided filter algorithm.

title: Edge-Preserving Guided Filter for Volumetric Images — guided_filter
text: , K., Sun, J., & Tang, X. (2013). Guided Image Filtering. IEEE Transactions Pattern Analysis Machine Intelligence, 35(6), 1397-1409.

title: Edge-Preserving Guided Filter for Volumetric Images — guided_filter
code: # Load an example brain volume brain_vol <- read_vol(system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package = "neuroim2"))  # Apply guided filtering to the brain volume # \donttest{ filtered_vol <- guided_filter(brain_vol, radius = 4, epsilon = 0.49)  # Visualize a slice of the original and filtered volumes oldpar <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) image(brain_vol[,,12], main = "Original") image(filtered_vol[,,12], main = "Filtered")  par(oldpar) # }

TR Repetition time (4th pixdim), NA 4D. raw complete raw header list (NIfTI fields).

what: Build smooth time weights from motion/outlier metrics — make_time_weights
title: Build smooth time weights from motion/outlier metrics — make_time_weights
text: Creates per-time-point weights \(w_t \[0, 1]\) smoothly combining framewise displacement (FD), DVARS, spike/outlier scores. series transformed soft logistic ramp values beyond specified thresholds receive progressively lower weights instead hard 0/1 decisions.

title: Build smooth time weights from motion/outlier metrics — make_time_weights
code: make_time_weights(   fd = NULL,   dvars = NULL,   spike = NULL,   fd_thr = 0.5,   dvars_z = 2.5,   spike_z = 5,   fd_soft = 0.1,   dvars_soft = 0.25,   combine = c("min", "prod") )

title: Build smooth time weights from motion/outlier metrics — make_time_weights
text: fd Optional numeric vector framewise displacement values. dvars Optional numeric vector DVARS values. spike Optional numeric vector spike/outlier magnitudes. fd_thr Threshold (mm) FD weights start drop (default 0.5). dvars_z Z-threshold applied standardized DVARS series (default 2.5). spike_z Z-threshold applied standardized spike series (default 5). fd_soft Logistic softness (mm) controlling slope around fd_thr. dvars_soft Logistic softness DVARS z-scores. combine Either "min" (take minimum weight per TR) "prod" (multiply weights).

title: Build smooth time weights from motion/outlier metrics — make_time_weights
text: Numeric vector weights \([0,1]\) length equal   provided series. least one fd, dvars, spike   must supplied.

The first column is the key, and the second column is the value names(lookup) <- 1:length(lookup) lookup.mat <- cbind(as.numeric(names(lookup)), unlist(lookup)) ovol2 <- map_values(vol, lookup.mat) all.equal(as.vector(ovol2), as.vector(ovol)) #> [1] TRUE

title: Extract values from a 4D tensor using a matrix of time-space indices. — matricized_access
text: matrix, returns numeric vector values specified time-space coordinates.         vector, returns matrix column contains full time series spatial index.

title: Extract values from a 4D tensor using a matrix of time-space indices. — matricized_access
code: # Create a sparse 4D neuroimaging vector bspace <- NeuroSpace(c(10,10,10,100), c(1,1,1)) mask <- array(rnorm(10*10*10) > .5, c(10,10,10)) mat <- matrix(rnorm(sum(mask)), 100, sum(mask)) svec <- SparseNeuroVec(mat, bspace, mask)  # Extract specific timepoint-voxel pairs # Get value at timepoint 1, voxel 1 and timepoint 2, voxel 2 idx_mat <- matrix(c(1,1, 2,2), ncol=2, byrow=TRUE) vals <- matricized_access(svec, idx_mat)  # Get full time series for voxels 1 and 2 ts_mat <- matricized_access(svec, c(1,2)) # Each column in ts_mat contains the full time series for that voxel

title: Convert a Transformation Matrix to a Quaternion Representation — matrixToQuatern
text: function first checks corrects zero-length axes upper-left corner matrix, normalizes column extract pure rotation. determinant rotation submatrix negative, qfac set -1, third column negated. Finally, quaternion parameters (\(, b, c, d\)) computed following standard NIfTI-1 conventions representing rotation 3D.

title: Lightweight metadata for neuroimaging files — meta_info
text: named list following elements: `dim` Integer vector image dimensions. `spacing` Numeric voxel spacing (mm). `origin` Numeric coordinate origin. `trans` 4x4 transformation matrix mapping grid world (mm). `path` Data file path. `filename` Basename `path`. `format` File format label (e.g., "NIFTI", "AFNI"). `dtype` Storage data type label. `bytes_per_element` Bytes per element. `nvox` Number voxels spatial volume (prod first 3 dims). `nvol` Number volumes (4th dim present, else 1). `size_bytes` Approximate uncompressed size bytes (`nvox * nvol * bytes_per_element`). `time_step` Time step (TR seconds) available NIfTI, else `NA_real_`.

title: Number of Clusters — num_clusters
code: # Create a simple 3D volume and mask space <- NeuroSpace(c(16, 16, 16), spacing = c(1, 1, 1)) vol_data <- array(rnorm(16^3), dim = c(16, 16, 16)) mask_vol <- LogicalNeuroVol(vol_data > 0, space)  # Get coordinates of masked voxels for clustering mask_idx <- which(mask_vol) coords <- index_to_coord(mask_vol, mask_idx)  # Cluster the coordinates into 10 groups using k-means set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility kmeans_result <- kmeans(coords, centers = 10)  # Create a clustered volume clustered_vol <- ClusteredNeuroVol(mask_vol, kmeans_result$cluster)  # Get the number of clusters n_clusters <- num_clusters(clustered_vol) n_clusters == 10 #> [1] TRUE

title: Orientation utility functions — orientation_utils
text: Helper functions converting affine matrices, axis-code representations, array orientation transforms. Returns orientation transform maps `start_ornt` `end_ornt`. Applies flips permutations implied `ornt` first `n` dimensions `arr`, `n = nrow(ornt)`. Returns affine mapping transformed array coordinates back source array coordinates transform encoded `ornt`.

title: Orientation utility functions — orientation_utils
code: affine_to_orientation(affine, tol = NULL)  orientation_transform(start_ornt, end_ornt)  apply_orientation(arr, ornt)  orientation_inverse_affine(ornt, shape)  orientation_to_axcodes(ornt, labels = NULL)  axcodes_to_orientation(axcodes, labels = NULL)  affine_to_axcodes(affine, labels = NULL, tol = NULL)

title: Orientation utility functions — orientation_utils
text: affine Affine matrix. tol Optional singular-value tolerance. start_ornt Starting orientation matrix. end_ornt Target orientation matrix. arr Array-like object. ornt Orientation matrix. shape Shape source array. labels Optional label pairs per axis. axcodes Character vector axis codes. `NA` indicates dropped axis.

title: Orientation utility functions — orientation_utils
text: `p x 2` orientation matrix columns `axis` `flip`. Orientation matrix representing `start -> end`. Reoriented array. Homogeneous affine matrix size `(p + 1) x (p + 1)`. Character vector axis codes (positive ends), `NA` dropped axes. Orientation matrix. Character vector axis codes.

title: Orientation utility functions — orientation_utils
text: Orientation matrices (`ornt`) use two columns: column 1 (`axis`) stores output axis index (1-based), column 2 (`flip`) stores direction (`1` `-1`). R counterpart NiBabel's orientation utilities, adapted `neuroim2` conventions.

title: Orientation utility functions — orientation_utils
code: aff <- diag(4) ornt <- affine_to_orientation(aff) orientation_to_axcodes(ornt) #> [1] "R" "A" "S"  arr <- array(1:24, dim = c(2, 3, 4)) tx <- orientation_transform(   axcodes_to_orientation(c("R", "A", "S")),   axcodes_to_orientation(c("A", "R", "S")) ) out <- apply_orientation(arr, tx)  inv_aff <- orientation_inverse_affine(tx, dim(arr)) inv_aff #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] #> [1,]    0    1    0    0 #> [2,]    1    0    0    0 #> [3,]    0    0    1    0 #> [4,]    0    0    0    1

what: Prepare weighted nuisance projectors for each run — prepare_confounds
title: Prepare weighted nuisance projectors for each run — prepare_confounds
text: Converts per-run confound matrices time weights orthonormal projectors can consumed directly C++ graph builder. run produces \(Q_k\) (columns spanning weighted confound space) \(\sqrt{w_k}\) (per-time-point square-root weights). Supplying NULL confound matrix yields zero-column projector, enabling pure time-weighting without regression.

title: Prepare weighted nuisance projectors for each run — prepare_confounds

title: Prepare weighted nuisance projectors for each run — prepare_confounds
text: confounds List matrices (\(T_k \times p_k\)), single matrix reused runs. row corresponds time point. time_weights Optional list numeric vectors (length \(T_k\)) single vector reused every run. NULL, unit weights used. run_lengths Integer vector number time points per run. Required run confounds=NULL time_weights=NULL. include_intercept Logical; prepend column ones QR (default TRUE). center_cols Logical; center confound column weighting (default TRUE). scale_cols Logical; scale columns unit variance weighting (default FALSE).

title: Prepare weighted nuisance projectors for each run — prepare_confounds

text: quat numeric vector length 3 containing quaternion parameters \((b, c, d)\). scalar part \(\) computed internally. origin numeric vector length 3 specifying translation components (often real-space origin offset). stepSize numeric vector length 3 giving voxel dimensions along axis (e.g., (dx, dy, dz)). qfac Either +1 -1, indicating sign determinant check matrixToQuatern.

title: Convert Quaternion Parameters to a Transformation Matrix — quaternToMatrix
text: 4x4 numeric affine transformation matrix. top-left 3x3 submatrix   encodes rotation scaling, 4th column encodes translation.

title: Convert Quaternion Parameters to a Transformation Matrix — quaternToMatrix
text: function uses quaternion formalism common neuroimaging, adding offset (translation) 4th column, applying voxel sizes along axis. qfac -1, \(z\) scale negated. resulting 4x4 matrix typically used affine transform voxel--world coordinate mapping.

title: read_image — read_image
text: Convenience wrapper inspects file header(s) dispatches appropriate specialized reader: read_vol single 3D file, read_vec 4D data multi-file input, read_hyper_vec 5D data.

title: read_image — read_image
code: read_image(   file_name,   type = c("auto", "vol", "vec", "hyper"),   index = 1,   indices = NULL,   mask = NULL,   mode = c("normal", "mmap", "bigvec", "filebacked") )

title: read_image — read_image
text: file_name Character vector one file paths. type One "auto", "vol", "vec", "hyper" override header-based dispatch. index Integer volume index. Used single-volume selector read_vol (dispatching ) , indices NULL, forwarded read_vec indices can pull subset time points still receiving NeuroVec. indices Optional integer vector sub-volume indices forwarded read_vec. Takes precedence index. mask Optional spatial mask passed vector hyper-vector readers. Ignored read_vol. mode IO mode forwarded read_vec; see function details. Ignored 3D 5D dispatch.

title: read_image — read_image
text: return type depends dispatch: 3D dispatch (single effectively-3D file, type = "vol"):         DenseNeuroVol. 4D dispatch (single 4D file, type = "vec" one file):         NeuroVec (concrete subclass depends mode). Multi-file dispatch (length(file_name) > 1,         type = "vec" multiple files):         NeuroVecSeq, extends NeuroVec stores         file distinct segment rather concatenating single         contiguous 4D array. Mixed 3D/4D inputs allowed; 3D file         contributes one time point sequence. 5D dispatch (single 5D file, type = "hyper"):         NeuroHyperVec.

title: read_image — read_image
text: Auto-dispatch (type = "auto") uses following rules: length(file_name) > 1: always routed read_vec,         result NeuroVecSeq regardless         whether individual files 3D, 4D, mix. See         read_vec return-type details. Single file 5th dimension > 1: routed         read_hyper_vec, returning NeuroHyperVec. Single file 4th dimension > 1: routed         read_vec, returning NeuroVec.         index supplied (indices ), forwarded         indices can pull subset still getting         NeuroVec back. Single effectively-3D file (either truly 3D 4D dim[4] == 1):         routed read_vol, returning DenseNeuroVol. Explicit type values bypass header inspection: type = "vol": requires single file; always returns         DenseNeuroVol. index argument picks         sub-volume file 4D. type = "vec": forwards read_vec. Multi-file input         yields NeuroVecSeq. single 3D file promoted         NeuroVec dim[4] == 1. type = "hyper": requires single file; returns         NeuroHyperVec.

title: read_image — read_image
code: # 3D file -> DenseNeuroVol vol <- read_image(system.file("extdata", "global_mask2.nii.gz", package = "neuroim2"))  # 4D file -> NeuroVec vec <- read_image(system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package = "neuroim2"))  # Multiple files (any mix of 3D and 4D) -> NeuroVecSeq fn <- system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package = "neuroim2") seq_vec <- read_image(c(fn, fn)) class(seq_vec)  # "NeuroVecSeq" #> [1] "NeuroVecSeq" #> attr(,"package") #> [1] "neuroim2"

title: read_vec — read_vec
text: Loads neuroimaging volume one files, support various input formats memory management strategies.

title: read_vec — read_vec
code: read_vec(   file_name,   indices = NULL,   mask = NULL,   mode = c("normal", "mmap", "bigvec", "filebacked") )

title: read_vec — read_vec
text: file_name character vector one file paths load. 3D file promoted 4D NeuroVec single time point (see Details). multiple paths supplied result always NeuroVecSeq (extends NeuroVec), regardless whether individual files 3D, 4D, mix . indices indices sub-volumes load (e.g. file 4-dimensional). supported "normal" mode. mask logical mask defining spatial elements load. Required "bigvec" mode optional modes. mode IO mode one : * "normal": Standard -memory loading * "mmap": Memory-mapped access (memory efficient) * "bigvec": Optimized large datasets masking * "filebacked": File-backed storage -demand loading

title: read_vec — read_vec
text: return type depends many files supplied: Single 3D file — NeuroVec         dim(x)[4] == 1 (concrete class depends mode: e.g.         DenseNeuroVec "normal",         MappedNeuroVec "mmap",         BigNeuroVec "bigvec",         FileBackedNeuroVec "filebacked"). Single 4D file — NeuroVec         concrete class , dim(x)[4] equal 4th dimension         file (length(indices) indices supplied). Multiple files (mix 3D 4D) —         NeuroVecSeq wrapping one NeuroVec per input         file order given. NeuroVecSeq extends         NeuroVec, can used wherever NeuroVec         accepted, underlying storage segmented rather single         contiguous 4D array. example, read_vec(c(vol, vec, vec, vol))         returns 4-element NeuroVecSeq whose segments time lengths         c(1, T2, T3, 1).

title: read_vec — read_vec
text: function supports multiple file formats: * .nii: Standard NIfTI format * .nii.gz: Compressed NIfTI (supported mmap mode) Memory management modes: * "normal": Loads entire dataset memory. Best smaller datasets memory   constraint. * "mmap": Memory-maps file, providing efficient access large files without   loading entirely memory. available compressed files. * "bigvec": Optimized large datasets subset voxels interest.   Requires mask specify voxels load. * "filebacked": Similar mmap flexible caching strategies. 3D inputs: path pointing 3D image rejected. promoted 4D NeuroVec whose fourth dimension length 1, return type always NeuroVec, never NeuroVol. want true volume, use read_vol (vec[[1]]). Multiple files: file_name length > 1, file loaded independently results wrapped NeuroVecSeq. data copied re-concatenated single dense 4D array; returned NeuroVecSeq holds constituent NeuroVecs @vecs slot exposes single logical time series. Time-point lookups (e.g. x[[]], sub_vector(x, ), linear_access(x, )) transparently walk across segments. per-file time lengths may differ (e.g. 3D file contributes 1 time point, 4D file contributes dim(file)[4]); spatial dimensions must match.

title: read_vec — read_vec
text: * Memory-mapping (.mmap mode) supported gzipped files * bigvec mode requires mask specified * loading multiple files, must compatible dimensions

title: read_vec — read_vec
code: # Load a single NIfTI file img <- read_vec(system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package="neuroim2"))   # Memory-mapped loading for large files big_img <- read_vec(system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package="neuroim2"), mode="mmap") #> loading /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/neuroim2/extdata/global_mask_v4.nii as mmaped file   # Load masked data for memory efficiency mask <- as.logical(big_img[[1]]) masked_data <- read_vec(system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package="neuroim2"),                mask=mask, mode="bigvec") #> loading /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/neuroim2/extdata/global_mask_v4.nii as bigvec

text: x NeuroSpace object, orient argument character vector length 3 specifying desired anatomical orientation using single-letter codes. letter represents anatomical direction: First position: "R" (Right) "L" (Left) Second position: "" (Anterior) "P" (Posterior) Third position: "S" (Superior) "" (Inferior) example, c("R", "", "S") specifies Right-Anterior-Superior orientation, c("L", "P", "") specifies Left-Posterior-Inferior orientation. orientation codes determine voxel grid coordinates map real-world anatomical space.

title: Remap the grid-to-world coordinates mapping of an image. — reorient
code: # Create a NeuroSpace object in LPI (Left-Posterior-Inferior) orientation space <- NeuroSpace(c(64, 64, 40), c(2, 2, 2))  # Reorient to RAS (Right-Anterior-Superior) orientation # Use individual axis codes: "R" for Right, "A" for Anterior, "S" for Superior space_ras <- reorient(space, c("R", "A", "S"))  # The transformation matrix will be updated to reflect the new orientation # Original and reoriented spaces will have different coordinate mappings coords <- c(32, 32, 20) orig_world <- grid_to_coord(space, coords) new_world <- grid_to_coord(space_ras, coords)

what: Create a resampled searchlight iterator — resampled_searchlight
title: Create a resampled searchlight iterator — resampled_searchlight
text: function generates resampled searchlight iterator sampling regions within brain mask. default builds spherical searchlights, users can provide custom shape_fun return ellipsoids, cubes, arbitrary irregular searchlight shapes. Centers drawn replacement, voxel (neighborhood) may appear multiple times. searchlight can also draw radius user-specified set radii.

title: Create a resampled searchlight iterator — resampled_searchlight
code: resampled_searchlight(   mask,   radius = 8,   iter = 100,   shape_fun = NULL,   nonzero = TRUE )  bootstrap_searchlight(mask, radius = 8, iter = 100)

title: Create a resampled searchlight iterator — resampled_searchlight
text: mask NeuroVol object representing brain mask. radius numeric scalar vector specifying candidate radii (voxel units) searchlight sphere. vector supplied, radius sampled uniformly (replacement) searchlight. radii must positive. Default 8. iter integer specifying total number searchlights sample (replacement). Default 100. shape_fun Either NULL (default spherical kernel), character keyword ("sphere", "ellipsoid", "cube", "blobby"), custom function. Custom functions called shape_fun(mask, center, radius, iter, nonzero) must return either ROIVolWindow n x 3 integer matrix voxel coordinates. enables anisotropic irregular searchlights. nonzero Logical; TRUE (default), generated searchlight intersected non-zero voxels mask. Applies default sphere shape_fun returns coordinates.

title: Create a resampled searchlight iterator — resampled_searchlight
text: deferred_list object containing ROIVolWindow   objects, representing sampled searchlight region drawn within mask.

title: Create a resampled searchlight iterator — resampled_searchlight
text: Searchlight centers sampled replacement, center (surrounding voxels) can selected multiple times. multiple radii provided, searchlight independently samples one radius supplied values. Supplying shape_fun lets draw non-spherical searchlights (e.g., ellipsoids, cubes, blobby deformations, task-specific kernels). Built-shortcuts available via shape_fun = "ellipsoid", "cube", "blobby"; "sphere" NULL uses default spherical kernel.

title: Create a resampled searchlight iterator — resampled_searchlight
code: # Load an example brain mask mask <- read_vol(system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package="neuroim2"))  # Generate a resampled searchlight iterator with radii drawn from {4,6,8} searchlights <- resampled_searchlight(mask, radius = c(4, 6, 8))  # Use a custom shape: random ellipsoid scaled along each axis ellipsoid_fun <- function(mask, center, radius, iter, nonzero) {   scales <- runif(3, 0.5, 1.5)        # axis-wise stretch/compress   vox <- spherical_roi(mask, center, radius, nonzero = FALSE)@coords   ctr_mat <- matrix(center, nrow(vox), 3, byrow = TRUE)   keep <- rowSums(((vox - ctr_mat) * scales)^2) <= radius^2   vox[keep, , drop = FALSE] } ellip_searchlights <- resampled_searchlight(mask, radius = c(4, 6),                                             iter = 50, shape_fun = ellipsoid_fun)  # Or use built-in named shapes ellip_builtin <- resampled_searchlight(mask, radius = 6, shape_fun = "ellipsoid") cube_builtin  <- resampled_searchlight(mask, radius = 6, shape_fun = "cube")

Values >1 stretch; <1 compress. jitter Non-negative numeric; standard deviation multiplicative Gaussian noise applied scales draw (ellipsoid). drop Numeric [0,1]; probability dropping voxel (blobby). edge_fraction Numeric (0,1]; fraction farthest voxels (Euclidean distance center, voxel units) considered "edge" eligible random dropping (blobby).

code: mask <- read_vol(system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package="neuroim2"))  # Ellipsoid stretched along z with modest per-iteration jitter sl_ellip <- resampled_searchlight(mask, radius = 6,                                    shape_fun = ellipsoid_shape(scales = c(1, 1, 1.4),                                                               jitter = 0.1))  # Simple axis-aligned cube (Chebyshev ball) sl_cube <- resampled_searchlight(mask, radius = 5, shape_fun = "cube")  # Blobby sphere with 40% dropout on boundary voxels sl_blob <- resampled_searchlight(mask, radius = 6,                                  shape_fun = blobby_shape(drop = 0.4, edge_fraction = 0.6))

title: Extract one or more series from object — series
code: # Create a simple 4D neuroimaging vector (10x10x10 volume with 20 timepoints) space <- NeuroSpace(c(10,10,10,20), c(1,1,1)) vec <- NeuroVec(array(rnorm(10*10*10*20), c(10,10,10,20)), space)  # Extract time series using linear indices ts1 <- series(vec, 1:10)  # Get time series for first 10 voxels  # Extract time series using 3D coordinates coords <- matrix(c(1,1,1, 2,2,2, 3,3,3), ncol=3, byrow=TRUE) ts2 <- series(vec, coords)  # Get time series for 3 specific voxel locations  # Extract single time series using x,y,z coordinates ts3 <- series(vec, 5, 5, 5)  # Get time series from middle voxel

title: Simulate fMRI Data — simulate_fmri
text: Generates synthetic 4D fMRI data realistic spatiotemporal properties including temporal autocorrelation, spatial smoothness, heteroscedasticity, optional global signal fluctuations latent components.

title: Simulate fMRI Data — simulate_fmri
code: simulate_fmri(   mask,   n_time,   TR = 2,   spatial_fwhm = 6,   ar_mean = 0.45,   ar_sd = 0.08,   noise_sd = 1,   hetero_fwhm = 20,   hetero_strength = 0.6,   global_amp = 0.2,   global_rho = 0.85,   n_factors = 4,   factor_fwhm = 12,   factor_rho = 0.8,   seed = NULL,   return_centered = TRUE )

title: Simulate fMRI Data — simulate_fmri
text: mask NeuroVol object defining brain mask region. Can binary continuous (non-zero values define mask). n_time Integer specifying number time points simulate. TR Numeric value repetition time seconds (default = 2.0). Currently used metadata. spatial_fwhm Numeric value specifying spatial smoothness mm (full width half maximum) applied timepoint (default = 6). ar_mean Numeric value mean AR(1) coefficient distribution across voxels (default = 0.45). ar_sd Numeric value standard deviation AR(1) coefficient distribution (default = 0.08). noise_sd Numeric value baseline noise standard deviation (default = 1.0). hetero_fwhm Numeric value spatial scale (FWHM mm) heteroscedasticity field (default = 20). hetero_strength Numeric value controlling strength spatial heteroscedasticity log scale (default = 0.6). global_amp Numeric value amplitude global signal fluctuations fraction median noise (default = 0.2). Set 0 disable. global_rho Numeric value AR(1) coefficient global signal (default = 0.85). n_factors Integer specifying number latent spatial components (default = 4). Set 0 disable. factor_fwhm Numeric value spatial smoothness (FWHM mm) latent component maps (default = 12). factor_rho Numeric value AR(1) coefficient latent component time courses (default = 0.8). seed Integer seed random number generation (default = NULL seed). return_centered Logical indicating whether center voxel's time series mean zero (default = TRUE).

title: Simulate fMRI Data — simulate_fmri
text: NeuroVec object containing simulated 4D fMRI data.

title: Simulate fMRI Data — simulate_fmri
text: simulation combines several realistic features: Voxel-wise AR(1) temporal autocorrelation spatial variation Spatial smoothing applied innovations realistic spatial correlation Heteroscedastic noise smooth spatial modulation Optional low-frequency global signal fluctuations Optional latent spatial components resembling resting-state networks spatial smoothing uses package's optimized gaussian_blur function efficiency.

title: Simulate fMRI Data — simulate_fmri
text: Welvaert, M., & Rosseel, Y. (2013). definition signal--noise ratio contrast--noise ratio fMRI data. PloS one, 8(11), e77089.

title: Simulate fMRI Data — simulate_fmri
code: # Create a simple spherical mask dims <- c(32, 32, 20) mask_array <- array(FALSE, dims) center <- dims / 2 for (i in 1:dims[1]) {   for (j in 1:dims[2]) {     for (k in 1:dims[3]) {       if (sum(((c(i,j,k) - center) / (dims/3))^2) <= 1) {         mask_array[i,j,k] <- TRUE       }     }   } }  mask <- NeuroVol(mask_array, NeuroSpace(dims, c(3,3,3)))  # Simulate 100 time points sim_data <- simulate_fmri(mask, n_time = 100, seed = 42)  # Check dimensions dim(sim_data)  # Should be c(32, 32, 20, 100) #> [1]  32  32  20 100

code: # Create a NeuroSpace object with dimensions (10, 10, 10) and voxel size (1, 1, 1) x <- NeuroSpace(c(10, 10, 10), c(1, 1, 1))  # Create a NeuroVol object with random data and the specified NeuroSpace vol <- NeuroVol(rnorm(10 * 10 * 10), x)  # Retrieve the geometric properties of the NeuroVol object identical(x, space(vol)) #> [1] TRUE  space <- NeuroSpace(c(64, 64, 64), c(1, 1, 1), c(0, 0, 0)) ilv <- IndexLookupVol(space, c(1:100)) space(ilv)  # Get the associated NeuroSpace object #> <NeuroSpace> [3D]  #> ── Geometry ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   Dimensions    : 64 x 64 x 64 #>   Spacing       : 1 x 1 x 1 mm #>   Origin        : 0, 0, 0 #>   Orientation   : RAS #>   Voxels        : 262,144

text: instance class ROIVol

title: Create a Spherical Region of Interest — spherical_roi
code: sp1 <- NeuroSpace(c(10,10,10), c(1,2,3))  # create an ROI centered around the integer-valued positive voxel coordinate: i=5, j=5, k=5  cube <- spherical_roi(sp1, c(5,5,5), 3.5)  vox <- coords(cube)  cds <- coords(cube, real=TRUE)  ## fill in ROI with value of 6  cube1 <- spherical_roi(sp1, c(5,5,5), 3.5, fill=6)  all(cube1 == 6) #> [1] TRUE   ## Create multiple spherical ROIs at once (preferred):  centers <- rbind(c(5,5,5), c(3,3,3), c(7,7,7))  vols <- spherical_roi_set(bvol = sp1,                           centroids = centers, radius = 3.5, fill = 1)  length(vols)  # 3 #> [1] 3   ## Equivalent, less efficient lapply variant:  vols2 <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(centers)), function(i) {    spherical_roi(sp1, centers[i,], radius = 3.5, fill = 1)  })   # create an ROI centered around the real-valued coordinates: x=5, y=5, z=5  vox <- coord_to_grid(sp1, c(5, 5, 5))  cube <- spherical_roi(sp1, vox, 3.5)

title: Cut an object into a list of spatial or spatiotemporal clusters — split_clusters
text: list sub-objects, sub-object corresponds unique cluster index. deflist (lazy-loading list) ROIVec objects, element corresponds cluster.

title: Cut an object into a list of spatial or spatiotemporal clusters — split_clusters
text: two methods splitting clusters: Using ClusteredNeuroVol object: method uses pre-defined clusters ClusteredNeuroVol object. Using integer vector: method allows custom cluster assignments. methods return deflist, lazy-loading list ROIVec objects.

title: Cut an object into a list of spatial or spatiotemporal clusters — split_clusters
code: # \donttest{   # Create a synthetic 3D volume and its NeuroSpace   space <- NeuroSpace(c(10, 10, 10,4))   vol_data <- array(rnorm(10 * 10 * 10 * 4), dim = c(10, 10, 10,4))   neuro_vec <- NeuroVec(vol_data, space)    # Create a binary mask (e.g., select voxels with values > 0)   mask_data <- as.logical(neuro_vec[[1]] > .5)   mask_vol <- LogicalNeuroVol(mask_data, NeuroSpace(c(10, 10, 10)))    # Extract indices and coordinates for the masked voxels   mask_idx <- which(mask_data)   coords <- index_to_coord(mask_vol, mask_idx)    # Perform k-means clustering on the coordinates (e.g., 3 clusters)   set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility   k_res <- kmeans(coords, centers = 3)    # Create a ClusteredNeuroVol using the mask and k-means cluster assignments   clustered_vol <- ClusteredNeuroVol(mask_vol, k_res$cluster)    # Split the NeuroVec by clusters using the ClusteredNeuroVol method   split_result_clust <- split_clusters(neuro_vec, clustered_vol)    # Calculate and print the mean value for each cluster   means_clust <- sapply(split_result_clust, function(x) mean(values(x)))   print(means_clust) #> [1] 0.3150001 0.2943747 0.3140765    # Alternatively, create an integer vector of cluster assignments:   cluster_assignments <- numeric(prod(dim(space)[1:3]))   cluster_assignments[mask_idx] <- k_res$cluster   split_result_int <- split_clusters(neuro_vec, as.integer(cluster_assignments))    # Verify that both splitting methods yield the same cluster means   means_int <- sapply(split_result_int, function(x) mean(values(x)))   print(all.equal(sort(means_clust), sort(means_int))) #> [1] TRUE # }   # Create a simple example space and data space <- NeuroSpace(c(10, 10, 10,4)) data <- array(rnorm(1000*4), dim = c(10, 10, 10,4)) vec <- NeuroVec(data, space)  # Create a mask for clustering (e.g., values > 0) mask <- vec[,,,1] > 0 mask_vol <- LogicalNeuroVol(as.array(mask), NeuroSpace(c(10, 10, 10)))  # Get coordinates of masked voxels for clustering mask_idx <- which(mask) coords <- index_to_coord(mask_vol, mask_idx)  # Perform clustering on the coordinates (3 clusters for example) set.seed(123) # for reproducibility kmeans_result <- kmeans(coords, centers = 3)  # Create a ClusteredNeuroVol clustered_vol <- ClusteredNeuroVol(mask_vol, kmeans_result$cluster)  # Split the NeuroVec by clusters split_result <- split_clusters(vec, clustered_vol)  # Calculate mean value for each cluster cluster_means <- sapply(split_result, function(x) mean(values(x))) print(cluster_means) #> [1] 0.1735834 0.1777742 0.2143060  # Alternative: using integer cluster assignments cluster_indices <- numeric(prod(dim(space)[1:3])) cluster_indices[mask_idx] <- kmeans_result$cluster split_result2 <- split_clusters(vec, as.integer(cluster_indices))  # Verify both methods give same results cluster_means2 <- sapply(split_result2, function(x) mean(values(x))) print(all.equal(sort(cluster_means), sort(cluster_means2))) #> [1] TRUE

title: Summarize Subsets of an Object by Splitting by Row and Applying a Summary Function — split_reduce
code: mat = matrix(rnorm(100*100), 100, 100) fac = factor(sample(1:3, nrow(mat), replace=TRUE)) ## Compute column means of each sub-matrix ms <- split_reduce(mat, fac) all.equal(row.names(ms), levels(fac)) #> [1] TRUE  ## Compute column medians of each sub-matrix ms <- split_reduce(mat, fac, median)  ## Compute time-series means grouped over voxels. ## Here, 'length(fac)' must equal the number of voxels: 'prod(dim(bvec)[1:3])' bvec <- NeuroVec(array(rnorm(24*24*24*24), c(24,24,24,24)), NeuroSpace(c(24,24,24,24), c(1,1,1))) fac <- factor(sample(1:3, prod(dim(bvec)[1:3]), replace=TRUE)) ms <- split_reduce(bvec, fac) ms2 <- split_reduce(bvec, fac, mean) all.equal(row.names(ms), levels(fac)) #> [1] TRUE all.equal(ms, ms2) #> [1] TRUE

code: x <- NeuroSpace(c(10,10,10), spacing=c(1,1,1)) which_dim(x, x@axes@j) == 2 #> [1] TRUE

title: Write a sequence of elements from an input source — write_elements
code: # Create a temporary binary file for writing tmp <- tempfile() writer <- BinaryWriter(tmp, byte_offset = 0L,                       data_type = "DOUBLE", bytes_per_element = 8L)  # Write some random data data <- rnorm(100) write_elements(writer, data) close(writer)  # Read back the data to verify reader <- BinaryReader(tmp, byte_offset = 0L,                       data_type = "double", bytes_per_element = 8L) read_data <- read_elements(reader, 100) close(reader)  # Verify data was written correctly all.equal(data, read_data) #> [1] TRUE  # Clean up unlink(tmp) # \donttest{ # Create a temporary binary file for writing tmp <- tempfile() writer <- BinaryWriter(tmp, byte_offset = 0L,                       data_type = "DOUBLE", bytes_per_element = 8L) # Write some data write_elements(writer, rnorm(100)) close(writer)  # Clean up unlink(tmp) # }

title: neuroim2 0.12.0
text: plot_overlay() longer renders overlay flipped relative background. Previously overlay drawn via grid::rasterGrob, ignored slice’s affine transform, voxel (1,1) placed top-left panel background placed true world (mm) position. Overlays now reoriented match background span full pixel-edge extent. plot_overlay() longer errors “NAs allowed subscripted assignments” overlay contains NA voxels ov_thresh > 0. plot_overlay() panel titles now use x =, y =, z = based slicing axis (along).

title: neuroim2 0.12.0
text: NeuroVec now supports optional per-volume volume_labels() metadata across dense, sparse, mapped, file-backed, bigvec, NeuroVecSeq backends. Named volume access now supported via vec[["label"]], strict unique-match semantics, sub_vector() now accepts character label vectors. write_vec() now round-trips per-volume labels custom NIfTI extension; read_vec() low-footprint readers restore labels load.

title: neuroim2 0.12.0
text: Added focused tests per-volume label access, concatenation semantics, NeuroVecSeq, NIfTI label round-tripping.

title: neuroim2 0.11.0
text: Fixed NeuroSpace() derive spacing() origin() affine matrix constructed trans=. Previously, spacing() returned (1,1,1) spaces created explicit affine. Fixed drop_dim() 3D--2D NeuroSpace objects properly subset affine matrix, preserving spatial transforms instead lossy reconstruction spacing/origin.

title: neuroim2 0.11.0
text: Replaced crayon cli user-facing output. cli package provides structured error messages, progress bars, consistent ANSI formatting. Replaced assertthat cli::cli_abort() across 253 assertion sites, providing richer error messages argument class markup. Added vdiffr Suggests visual regression testing.

title: neuroim2 0.11.0
text: 28 show() methods now use unified formatting style via internal show_header()/show_rule()/show_field() helpers. New show() methods DenseNeuroVol NeuroSpace (previously informative display). random_searchlight() searchlight(eager=TRUE) now display cli progress bar interactive sessions. New normalize_mask() internal helper consolidates duplicated mask-coercion logic.

title: neuroim2 0.11.0
text: New NeuroSpace test suite (26 tests) covering construction, coordinate transforms, affine operations, dimension manipulation. New NIfTI /O round-trip tests (9 tests) verifying data/affine preservation across read-write cycles. New oblique affine regression tests (6 tests) downsample, resample, deoblique. New vdiffr plot snapshot tests (7 tests) plot(), plot_ortho(), plot_montage(), plot_overlay(). New shared test helper module factory functions (make_vol(), make_vec(), make_mask(), etc.).

title: neuroim2 0.11.0
text: New “Coordinate Systems Spatial Transforms” vignette explaining affine transforms, voxel/world coordinate conversion, orientation codes, common gotchas. Consolidated @rdname method families, reducing man pages 276 265.

title: neuroim2 0.10.0
text: Fixed downsample() DenseNeuroVol DenseNeuroVec output NeuroSpace objects now carry correctly rescaled affine transform. Previously, voxel dimensions change trans() still reflected pre-downsample grid.

title: neuroim2 0.9.1
text: plot_overlay() gains ov_alpha_mode argument: "binary" (default, existing behaviour) applies uniform alpha thresholded pixels, "proportional" scales per-pixel alpha absolute overlay value smoother blending. Internal helpers matrix_to_colors(), matrix_to_rgba(), matrix_to_raster_grob() now accept alpha_map argument support . Refactored orientation internals R/axis.R: new helpers .default_axcode_labels(), .validate_ornt(), ornt_transform(), inv_ornt_aff(), apply_orientation(), flip_axis(), io_orientation(), axcodes2ornt(), ornt2axcodes() provide comprehensive NiBabel-compatible orientation API. Plot colorbar guides improved better default labelling. Fixed dimension comments ClusteredNeuroVec vignette: searchlight values() dimensions documented N x T actually T x N (time points x neighbors). Removed stale albersdown dependency vignette setup chunks; vignettes now use neuroim2::theme_neuro() exclusively. Added .ecosystem.yml .Rbuildignore silence hidden-file NOTE. Added AFNI-style deoblique() NeuroSpace/NeuroVol, gridset/newgrid controls default isotropic grid spacing equal minimum input voxel size. NeuroVol inputs resampled axis-aligned deobliqued target space. Fixed plot(NeuroVol) plot(NeuroSlice) memory blowups oblique/sheared affines rasterizing pixel-grid coordinates instead world-coordinate grids; added regression tests oblique affine plotting.

title: neuroim2 0.9.0
text: Added 5D NIfTI support hyper-vectors: new read_hyper_vec() reader returns NeuroHyperVec; read_image(type = "auto") now dispatches NeuroHyperVec 5D inputs (optional spatial masking); write_vec() now supports NeuroHyperVec 5D NIfTI read/write round-trips supported. Fixed write_vec() affine round-trip regression: NIfTI sform (direct affine) now preferred qform (quaternion-derived) read, matching convention used FSL, FreeSurfer, ANTs. Previously, sform silently replaced qform, causing world-coordinate drift vector fields warp images. Fixed as_nifti_header() derive qoffset transform matrix translation column, ensuring internal header consistency quaternion parameters sform. Relaxed NeuroSpace affine precision 6 7 significant digits, matching NIfTI float32 precision reducing cumulative round-trip truncation.

title: neuroim2 0.8.7
text: New public orientation utility API: affine_to_orientation(), orientation_transform(), apply_orientation(), orientation_inverse_affine(), orientation_to_axcodes(), axcodes_to_orientation(), affine_to_axcodes(). New public space utility API: output_aligned_space() (NiBabel-compatible alias vox2out_vox()) slice_to_volume_affine() (alias slice2volume()), including support NeuroSpace/NeuroVol inputs, >3D spatial handling via first 3 axes, optional zero-based indexing compatibility. New public affine utility API: apply_affine(), to_matvec(), from_matvec(), append_diag(), dot_reduce(), voxel_sizes(), obliquity(), rescale_affine(), stricter validation center-preserving affine rescaling semantics. Fixed reorient(NeuroSpace, orient) returned spaces now carry requested orientation axes (instead preserving original axes). New sub_clusters() generic: subset ClusteredNeuroVol ClusteredNeuroVec integer cluster ID, numeric, character name (looked label map). Returns new object class containing selected clusters. scale_series() DenseNeuroVec now ~10x faster operating row-wise voxels--time matrix instead double-transposing base::scale. New dedicated scale_series() method SparseNeuroVec: scales masked voxels -place T×K @data matrix, returns 0 (NaN) zero-variance voxels, stays sparse. .dense() now identity (-copy) DenseNeuroVol. Arithmetic ops (+, -, *, /, ^) now work ClusteredNeuroVol (warning cluster structure preserved) scalar ops DenseNeuroVol SparseNeuroVol. Logic ops (&, |) negation (!) now work across DenseNeuroVol, SparseNeuroVol, LogicalNeuroVol, returning LogicalNeuroVol. Compare ops (>, <, ==, etc.) SparseNeuroVol DenseNeuroVol now correctly return LogicalNeuroVol instead raw sparse vectors mistyped volumes. Added NIfTI extension classes (NiftiExtension, NiftiExtensionList) read_image() convenience wrapper.

title: neuroim2 0.8.5
text: CRAN release: 2026-01-14 Windows build fix: added Makevars.win correctly link TBB libraries RcppParallel without non-existent -lRcppParallel flag. PDF manual fix: replaced Unicode characters (Greek letters, special symbols) roxygen documentation ASCII equivalents resolve LaTeX errors PDF generation.

title: neuroim2 0.8.4
text: /O reliability: enforce full-length binary reads (detects truncated images) ensure gz connections cleaned error. Data correctness: apply per-volume slope+intercept scaling consistently across NeuroVol/NeuroVec/SparseNeuroVec loaders (treat NIfTI slope==0 identity). Performance: SparseNeuroVecSource longer materializes full 4D arrays; reads masked voxels via mmap uncompressed files streams volumes sequentially gz files. Docs: fix missing Rd entries/aliases several exported functions S4 methods; remove vignette dependency albersdown.

title: neuroim2 0.8.3
text: Arithmetic/comparison fixes: SparseNeuroVec now unions masks keeps outputs sparse; sparse–sparse NeuroVol arithmetic returns SparseNeuroVol; numeric vs SparseNeuroVol comparison longer errors. 4D bilateral filter now measures intensity variance across timepoints mask skips non-finite neighbours, eliminating spurious NaNs constant noisy inputs. Added regression tests covering zero-window identity constant-volume stability parallel 4D bilateral filter backend. New meta_info() helper returns normalized list basic header metadata filename FileMetaInfo (dim, spacing, origin, trans, path, etc.), making 3D/4D image introspection simpler new users.

title: neuroim2 0.8.2
text: README refreshed: CRAN/R-universe install, CI/coverage badges, website & cheatsheet links. Docs: spherical_roi() now cross-links spherical_roi_set(); ROI vignette shows multi-ROI creation. New validity checks catch data/mask/space shape mismatches (#5). Robust .matrix.SparseNeuroVec() implementation (#2). New resample_to() wrapper readable interpolation names; delegates existing resample() methods.

title: neuroim2 0.8.1
text: CRAN release: 2025-04-10 Initial CRAN submission.
